element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns A button acceleration on relatively open track
Directional pad used to shift between four lanes to avoid obstacles
B button activates turbo but requires engine temperature management; introduces risk/reward
Cooldown periods force immobile waiting; reset by driving over arrows on track
Directional pad controls balance midair; landing squarely maintains momentum, uneven landing loses speed or crashes
Requires timed acceleration and jump distance judgment
Slows bike if hit; encourages lane avoidance strategy
Reduces tactical lane options; increases collision risk
Racing against AI; must qualify third place or higher in preliminary to reach championship
19 types of hurdles available for custom track creation; save/load via Famicom Data Recorder (JP only)

typical_relief_triggers: Landing squarely on both wheels maintaining momentum
Passing over arrow cooldown zones when engine overheated
Clear straightaway sections between obstacle clusters
typical_tension_triggers: Approaching upcoming gap or obstacle cluster
Uneven landing from jump reducing speed
Bottleneck sections with only two lanes
Computer opponents closing gap in Selection B
Rough patches on track requiring evasion
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating cycles of momentum building and precision-moment challenges; turbo boost creates risk/reward tension punctuated by forced cooldown relief periods; obstacle density increases as player progresses through track

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Design Mode exists as separate unlockable creative space from racing modes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Selection A solo race with relatively open track layout; introduces core mechanics (acceleration, lanes, jumping) in safe contexts
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive addition of obstacles (gaps, rough patches); increased density of terrain features requiring precise lane management and jump timing
difficulty_curve.late_game: Bottleneck sections reducing tactical lane options; Selection B competitive mode requiring third-place qualification; AI opponents create dynamic challenge
notable_spikes: First appearance of terrain gaps requiring timed jump execution
Bottleneck sections eliminating two lanes simultaneously
Selection B preliminary race qualification threshold

notable_level_patterns.description: Open track sections alternate with concentrated obstacle clusters (gaps, rough patches, narrow sections), creating natural tension/relief cycles
Tracks start with four available lanes and progressively reduce to two lanes in bottleneck sections, constraining player tactical options
Engine overheat mechanic creates natural rhythm breaks where player must pause forward progression, typically resolved by collecting arrow zones
Early jumps are simple terrain features; later stages introduce staggered/multiple consecutive jumps requiring sustained balance management
